name: Goldsky Blockchain Data Indexing API
what_it_does: Real-time blockchain data indexing platform with REST and GraphQL APIs — providing hosted subgraphs compatible with The Graph, live pipeline streaming, and mirror replication to PostgreSQL/data warehouses for multi-chain EVM event indexing.
use_cases: Running blockchain event indexing via hosted subgraphs compatible with The Graph
Streaming real-time EVM contract events to PostgreSQL for analytics agents
Replicating blockchain data to Redshift or BigQuery for data warehouse analysis
Indexing multi-chain NFT, DeFi, and token events from a single platform
Building blockchain analytics applications without managing indexer infrastructure
not_for: Non-EVM blockchains at the core — primarily EVM-compatible chains
Single query blockchain data without streaming (use QuickNode RPC)
best_when: A Web3 agent needs real-time EVM blockchain event indexing with SQL access or subgraph queries, without managing infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need Solana-specific high-performance indexing — use Helius or Shyft instead.

auth.notes: API key for platform management and subgraph deployment. GraphQL endpoint per subgraph (public or authenticated). CLI tool for pipeline and subgraph management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: limited indexing capacity and queries
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $99/month; Scale from $499/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Usage-based beyond free tier. Query-volume and data-volume pricing. Competitive with The Graph hosted service pricing. Pipeline streaming priced separately from subgraph queries.

performance.rate_limits: Query rate limits per plan; streaming latency near real-time (seconds behind chain)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good documentation for subgraph deployment and pipeline configuration. GraphQL introspection available per subgraph. Error responses from GraphQL follow spec. Pipeline streaming uses Kafka-compatible protocol.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All blockchain data queries are idempotent reads. Subgraph deployment is idempotent by version. Pipeline sync state is managed by Goldsky.
known_agent_gotchas: Subgraph deployment requires AssemblyScript knowledge for custom mapping logic
Live pipeline streaming data may be seconds behind chain tip — not truly real-time
Multi-chain indexing requires separate configuration per chain
GraphQL query complexity limits — paginate large result sets

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. No formal compliance certifications. Blockchain data is public — security focus is on API access control. US data residency.
